comments: TV mentions he saw ZZ Top 'on stage the other night' Hammersmith Odeon, London - Monday Oct 26, 1981. ZZ Top supported by Girl.
TV's introduction of the show's contents is missing, as is his show sign-off.

sessions.details: 1. Recorded for Friday Rock Show. Repeat TX.
Recorded on Saturday 29th August 1981 at the Reading Rock Festival.

track_listing.track: Bad Girl (LP - On The Rocks) Creole
Gonna Miss It When Its Gone (LP - This Is The Way) MCA

transcript.text: Now that's a track from the brand new Black Sabbath album. It's also out as a single. It's called The Mob Rules and before that you heard that very strange sort of spacey thing which has the title E5150.
And that is also on the Black Sabbath album which is coming out next week on Vertigo.
And before that something from Diary of a Madman, the latest album by Ozzy Osbourne and Blizzard of Oz. The track was called Flying High Again.
14 and a half minutes after 10, the Friday Rock Show.
From Radio 1, hello Gail and Val and Jackie and Miller Leaves and Mr. Wolseley Six and Co.
and Derek and Eggy and Bobby.
Little card in front of me that tells me that recently Gail had a 17th birthday.
I think last Tuesday. Happy birthday for them.
From this lady by the name of Wendy, hello to PC and all her mates in Belfast.
And also, I've got to say, watch out to a gentleman by the name of Paul
who is betrothed to Barbara L. Richards,
Mrs., she says in brackets.
Distinct feeling, Barbara,
that you're rather fond of Paul.
They live in Bradford.
I'd also like to say hello to some very good friends of mine
in my sort of spiritual home,
which is the town of Belfast in Northern Ireland.
To David and Peter and Mark and Liz.
And also to Sam and Mummy and Daddy
and not forgetting Caesar.
Bernie Marsden and friends.
It's a piece of music that first came out
way back in September 1977
on an album called Rain Dances.
But now you're going to find it on a compilation album that's just come out on Decca.
And it's a really good compilation of the music of Camel.
The album is called Chameleon, and that track there is entitled The Even Song.
Also on the album are Echoes and Song Within a Song, New, Drafted, Lies.
It's a really good compilation.
And before that, something from our repeat session by Bernie Marsden.
The track was called Shaky Ground.
It's 25 minutes after 10.
Nice little card here from a lady by the name of Jacqueline Millward,
who's studying at Birmingham University,
which is quite a long way from Newport in Gwent,
which is where her boyfriend is,
and I can't find his name anyway.
Do you want to see a card?
Do you know who you are?
Boyfriend of Jacqueline Milward?
She misses you?
She's looking forward to Christmas.
I said I can't hear from Wendy Davidson,
a 15 Danes-Crest Brompton, North Allerton, North Yorkshire.
Please give my crackpot but lovely with it husband Keith a mention, she says.
Wish him a very happy birthday for the 31st of October.
he's going to be the grand old age of 26.
It seems he never misses the programme, rather, for anything,
including Wendy, including me.
So if I give him a mention, there's a strong chance
that he might actually notice that I'm here too every Friday night
between 10pm and 12.
Happy birthday, Keith. Don't say hello to the missus.
That's Hawkwind. It's a track from their current album,
which is doing nicely in the charts, thank you very much.
The album called Sonic Attack.
The track you heard was called Streets of Fear.
Before that, something by the Pink Floyd, as if you didn't know.
From the album called The Wall, the track was comfortably numb.
Every time I hear that particular track, I think,
I wish that guitar solo would go on for the whole of the side of that record.
You just see him playing, it's sitting on top of the wall.
Magic.
Chris wants me to say hello to Mick and Melly,
who are incarcerated at the moment in Cumbria.
She's thinking of you.
Dune, the Sassanite from Scunthorpe, has a message here for Alison in Kilmarnock.
Tell her to grow her hair again.
and also tell her to go to Donington next year.
Hi from Janine, who is actually studying food technology,
she tells us, at Cannington College.
She's the lady who lives in Western Zoiland down there in Somerset.
And also somebody who knows Trenchard Barracks and Cellar,
which I know very well as well.
Damien was born in Preston,
but he wrote this letter from somewhere in Huntington.
Everybody he met at Buckton last summer,
especially Roger and Kieran and Malcolm and Tony and John and Joe
and Dave and Dave and Deidre and Alison.
Ooh, and loads of other people as well
Damien wants to be remembered to you all
It's 21 minutes to 11
Bernie Marsden and friends
Who's fooling who?
Now that's a band by the name of Grim Reaper
And it's a track you'll find on the compilation album
Called Heavy Metal Heroes
Which is out on the heavy metal label
And before that you heard something from our repeat session
By Bernie Marsden
It was called Who's Fooling Who
Also on that compilation album
You'll find Buffalo and Exposer
Split Beaver, Dragster, Witchfighter General
Well, it's actually a very, very good compilation of young, eager bands.
Gilliam Hardman for Oldham in Lancashire.
A happy birthday to her sister.
Her sister Judith is 21.
And also, hi to everybody who went on the barge.
That's David and John and Phil and Ruth and Caroline and Colin and Duncan,
most of whom, I'm told by Gilliam, are at university now.
Also, John Peake of Hitchin says say hello to brother David Peake
because he's resitting his O-Level exams in Llandudno in North Wales.
He's got a girlfriend by the name of Hazel Schotter, Haida Hazel.
And also to the lady that John was with the other night
in a Japanese restaurant in the centre of town,
a lady by the name of Mika Nakamura.
And John, yes, indeed, I was in that restaurant.
I was having udon suki, which sounds absolutely filthy,
but in actual fact is a really, really nice Japanese meal.
Good little single. It's out on Mercury.
It's by a band called Diesel.
Its title is Biked Back.
And before that, something from the Byron Band.
That's their first ever album.
It's out on Creole.
It's called On the Rocks.
The track was called Bad Girl.
Coming up to three minutes before 11,
theme one indicates the Friday Night Connection.
Right, last week, what did we play?
We played from In the Court of the Crimson King
by King Crimson, the track called Epitaph.
Then from Argus by Wishbone Ash,
The King Will Come.
and from Tres Hombres, who I saw on stage the other night, ZZ Top,
he played Sheik, as in Sheik of Araby.
Most people wrote in and said King was the answer.
We can't really accept that because King Crimson, that's okay,
the King will come, that's okay, but a Sheik or a Sheikh is not really a king,
he's a ruler.
We have to pick somebody out of the bag who says accurately what the answer is
and that particular somebody is somebody who lives around the corner from here, really.
In flat number two, 88 Sheffersbush Road, no.
Well, Hammersmith, London, W6.
Gentlemen, by the name of Alistair Anderson.
Mr. Anderson, you get yourself a £10 record token.
Well done.
And rarely do we ever get winners from London, too.
On the way to the Hammersmith Odeon next week to see whoever's on,
I'll pop in for a quick one.
So the answer to last week's Friday Night Connection was Rula.
Now, Alistair's put together a Friday Night Connection as well.
It's about 19 minutes long, and it starts off like this.
19 minutes after 11 o'clock, that's the Friday Night Connection for the night.
It was put together by Alistair Anderson.
Flat 2, 88 Shepherds Bush Road, Hammersmith, London, W6, Evan PJ.
Three bits of music, theme or connection contained therein.
Sitting here and cogitating, I was during that particular music and thinking,
wouldn't life be a drag if we didn't have music?
Now, if you reckon you know what the connection is,
just jot it down on a postcard or any form of communication you care to employ
and send it to The Friday Night Connection,
The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4, WW.
Don't forget, though, please, to put together your own Friday night connection as well.
And talking of things that you can get involved in, not that, not that, but this.
Our third birthday programme comes up quite soon, and if you care to put down...
This is an amendment to last week's announcement, incidentally.
If you care to put down your three all-time favourite rock tracks
on a separate bit of paper when you write in for something else,
or on a separate postcard, I'd be very, very grateful.
Your top three all-time favourite rock tracks.
If you're going to send them in on a separate piece of paper
in a separate envelope or postcard,
then if you could address it to Birthday,
Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4, WW.
I'd be more than grateful.
And when the birthday programme comes along,
then we'll correlate all the various bits and pieces
and bung it through the BBC computer and come out with a chart.
OK, so please, if you join in, I'd be grateful.
Your top three all-time rock tracks.
And address that to Birthday.
Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4, WWF.
It's 19 past 11.
And on airy are Neil Murray and Simon Phillips
and David Coverdale and Bernie Marston.
As on the front of the album, the free bird flies on.
I look forward to the day when they hit our shores.
The Rossing and Collins Band from the album
This Is The Way out on MCA.
the track you hope is going to miss it when it's gone.
Before that, something from our repeat session
by Bernie Marsden. The track was called
Look at Me Now. Dave Hewitt
says say hello to his girlfriend
Sue, who lives on Fairway Crescent
in Royton, which is near Oldham.
And he also wants to say a big thank you to all the
members of Hawkwind for the ace concert
that they all went to see in Manchester.
Also, Andy Gardner has
written to me and says say hi to Ant and Anne
and Daffy and Diff and Noddy and
Nick and everybody in Burgess Hill in West Sussex.
and a special happy birthday to Nick Miles
who had a birthday on the 27th of October
and Gerald Gardner
whose birthday comes up on the 2nd of November
Burgess Hill in Sussex
is the home of Nicky Hyde
who's written to me and says hello
say hello to Ben for me, okay
and Ratty, or maybe that's Ratty and Elsie
and Mark Newell and his friend Paul
and Beans and Deb and Gail and Jamie
and Simon and Sean and Liz
and Roger Day and his friend Brian
and everybody in St. Paul's 6th form, Haywood Seath
and with a bit of luck
A fellow by the name of Paddy Hall, who normally lives in Reading,
should at this moment of time be up there in Liverpool
in his friend's Lynn's house.
Paddy's gone up there to see Rush.
So he says, and demands that I say hello to Dobbs and Chip,
who should be up there in Liverpool with him by now.
And also, thank you very much to all the people that have assisted him in his journey,
because he's hitchhiked via Slough and then Watford
and then all the way up the motorway.
And according to his letter, he's going to be coming back down on Sunday.
Wish you well, Paddy.
The Friday Rock Show from Radio 1 is 29 and a half minutes after 11 of the clock.
And now we're going to go over for our live, well, recorded live at Reading piece of material by Ian Gillan and his band.
And I must say that I was in the audience at the time that Mr. Gillan was on stage and I thought they were magnificent.
And when it comes to doing the flying ballet bit, John McCoy has got to be just about the biggest star in the world, haven't you, Big John?
Okay, here's Gillan live at Reading.
There's a song called, I've been trying to get the, announce this properly.
This is called, again in French, it's called No Easy Way.
Well, I've got to tell you, it brings back some very nice memories to me, hearing that.
That was all recorded live at Reading this year.
The Ian Gillan Band are on stage.
On the rock she heard No Easy Way, No Laughing in Heaven, and that great intro that the band do.
A reminder that Ian and his band have got a double album out at the moment
that incorporates some of the material that was recorded live at Reading.
Six and a half minutes it is to midnight, the Friday Rock Show from BBC Radio 1.
Can I ask you to assist us in picking out our birthday programme this year?
What I'd like from you, please, is three rock tracks, your three all-time favourite rock tracks.
Tell them down a bit of paper, postcard, anything you like.
Think about it when you put it together, if you would.
There'll be three great rock tracks that you can think of.
Send your choice to Birthday, put that on the envelope or postcard,
And the Friday Rock Show, Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
Matt's birthday, Friday Rock Show, Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
Ta.
